chunk_summary: Guidance on household-level prescribing and adherence audits for THR and hot cooked meals to manage moderate acute malnutrition, emphasizing operational prescription, storage, preparation, and follow-up adherence checks.
learning_objectives: Understand the operational prescription of THR and hot cooked meals for MAM management.
Learn methods to ensure adherence to prescribed nutritional regimens at the household level.
Identify strategies to prevent sharing, over-dilution, and displacement of THR and HCM.
Develop skills to perform adherence audits and escalate programmatic issues effectively.
key_facts: THR and HCM should be prescribed operationally, like medication, rather than assuming receipt equals intake.
Caregivers should be instructed to treat the ration as the child’s medicine, with explicit storage and serving plans.
Preparation should aim for thick consistency with added fat to increase energy density, avoiding over-dilution.
Adherence audits should include checks on days received, days consumed by the child, sharing, storage, and preparation errors.
Escalation ladders should document issues like stockouts or poor-quality packets and involve AWC supervisors or CDPOs.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: ‘This ration is the child’s medicine.’ (Source: Supplementary Nutrition (ICDS) Rules, 2017)
document_key_topics: Prescribing THR and HCM for MAM
